problem_statement: Signed adapter worker receipts can report succeeded status, but known external-write capabilities need an inferred deterministic receipt boundary so the gateway cannot accept omitted, dry-run, or live-provider side-effect claims without retained proof.
success_criteria: Plan-only adapter receipts remain non-claiming and do not require provider receipt hashes
Dry-run execution success requires succeeded status, passed verification, evidence refs, idempotency key, and rollback or recovery ref
Known external-write capabilities fail closed when a succeeded receipt omits external-effect boundary evidence

Live-provider execution success requires an explicit external effect claim, retained provider observation hash/ref fields, idempotency key, rollback or recovery ref, approval ref, passed verification, false forbidden-effect observation, false secret-value disclosure, and evidence refs
Missing provider observation evidence is rejected with an explicit blocker code before a signed worker response can claim live-provider success
Non-boolean external effect, forbidden-effect, and secret-disclosure fields raise explicit errors
The assessment performs no provider network call, no request authentication, and no adapter credential mutation
non_goals: no live Gmail draft or send authority claim
no provider connector behavior change beyond receipt metadata binding
no deployment or production readiness claim
constraints: external effect success must be receipt-backed before gateway admission
plan-only worker output must not be treated as external execution success
provider observation hashes must use sha256-prefixed retained evidence
worker response signature validation must remain the first trust boundary
errors must be deterministic and auditable
evidence_required: focused adapter worker receipt-boundary test receipt
focused adapter worker serialization test receipt
standalone SDLC requirement and design validation receipt
workspace governance preflight receipt before closure claim
